item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thankee for the +1 and the feedback! I'll look over the skip when I get some time and see how to implement your feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the upvote!

I see where the confusion comes from. The other involved D-class involved is female. 4306 moved her out of the way when he initiated the time-stop but didn't put her back exactly where she was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minor issue: The Acquisition log refers to 4306 as "her" but he's established to be male. Otherwise I liked this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was rather slow through all the test logs, and I wasn't thrilled by the scip until the implications in the last line. +1, but this would be a lot better if it was heavily trimmed and focused on the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi there!

++ Minor Replies ++
-- Replies in a Minor Key --

Footnote 2 should probably be after "skin" and not "is"

I swapped that around, and it does flow better now! Thankee!

I'm not sure if the note about how the Janitor needs the Cleaning Items should be just after the table of cleaning items.

You may be right here. If I get more comments directing me toward changing its location, I definitely will.

Footnote 6 feels like it goes a little too hard on "this clock is never wrong"

I get what you're saying. The concept for Dr. Willitz is based around the college professor who is always late to everything and blames his alarm clock. The implication here being that the other Foundation researchers believed he is changing his clock to "prove" that he didn't realize he was late, and to eliminate the constant excuse, his supervisor had this clock specially designed.

… I feel like at this point it's well enough established that a third mention isn't necessary?

The third mention merely creates a narrative loop back to the UAI report. Basically, this establishes that Stan was touching KP's watch back in March, and KP wasn't even aware that Stan was the cause of the watch being three minutes fast the whole.

I appreciate the pointers, and will consider them more as time moves on!

Thanks for taking the time to read it and provide feedback! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this SCP and the time implications here :3

+ minor things
- despite the many words they really are minor i swear
  • Footnote 2 should probably be after "skin" and not "is"
  • I'm not sure if the note about how the Janitor needs the Cleaning Items should be just after the table of cleaning items. It makes narrative sense where it is but I stopped after reading the Cleaning Item table and was like "wait did I miss something about these? They're just cleaning things?" and moving it to there might help get around this?
  • Footnote 6 feels like it goes a little too hard on "this clock is never wrong"; I feel like the implications would still work even if it was just a regular old clock.
  • KP being all "It's been fast by about three minutes since March" feels weird cos like,,,why do they not just change it to be not fast? I get narratively that you're using this to emphasise the way the SCP alters time but I feel like at this point it's well enough established that a third mention isn't necessary?

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
My third article!

I wanna give a big shoutout to everyone who critiqued this draft!

Zyn helped me take this from a small, underdeveloped (yet overly complicated) idea to a completely different level where the complexity is balanced out with the character of Stan himself.

RockTeethMothEyes and gee0765 critiqued this draft in its second form (after gathering dust for about a year) and helped me clarify what the draft was going for!

You all are awesome and deserve anomalously tasty cookies!

By the way, I have blank UAI reports if anyone wants one.
